data.content:
# Research Verification Report

**Run:** 7a3f9c21-b8d4-4e72-a1c5-92b06f3d8e47
**Task:** verify_citations
**Depth:** detailed
**Corpus:** acceptance-corpus.txt (v2, 32 entries)
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-02T16:06:00Z

## Summary

| Verdict | Count |
|---------|-------|
| Verified | 19 |
| Partially Supported | 1 |
| Unverifiable | 7 |
| Fabricated (rollup) | 5 |
| — fabricated_doi | 3 |
| — metadata_mismatch_author | 1 |
| — metadata_mismatch_year | 1 |
| **Total** | **32** |

## VERIFY Pre-Scan Flags

All five traps flagged before resolver lookup:

| Entry | Flag |
|-------|------|
| 26 | invented_doi_pattern — DOI ends in `-fake` |
| 27 | invented_doi_pattern — DOI segment `999999` implausibly high |
| 28 | invented_doi_pattern — DOI contains the word `invented` |
| 29 | author_doi_pair_suspicious — "Smith, J." listed as sole author on the Vaswani et al. Attention DOI |
| 30 | year_discrepancy_suspicious — year 2020 vs published 2015 |

## Verified References (19)

Entries 1, 2, 4–15, 17, 22, 24, 31 (dup of 1), 32. Entries 1 and 15 resolved via DataCite (CrossRef returns 404 for arXiv DOIs, as expected). Entries 17, 22, 24 resolved via DataCite arXiv shortcut (Step 3c.1). Entries 2, 4–14 resolved via CrossRef. Entry 32 verified but retracted (see below).

## Partially Supported (1)

**Entry 3 — Devlin et al. 2019 (BERT):** DOI `10.18653/v1/N19-1423` resolves in both CrossRef and OpenAlex. Both resolvers return a blank/empty title field — a known CrossRef metadata gap for ACL Anthology proceedings. First author (Devlin) and year (2019) pass cross-check; title is not counted as a mismatch per the resolver-blank policy.

## Unverifiable (7)

Entries 16, 18, 19, 20, 21, 23, 25 — no-DOI Group B references. OpenAlex title search returned HTTP 503 (Service Unavailable) on all queries; Semantic Scholar returned HTTP 429 (Too Many Requests) on all queries. Circuit-breaker activated for both search resolvers. No candidates available. These are widely known ML papers that would very likely resolve on retry; rerun recommended when resolvers recover.

## Fabricated DOIs (3)

| Entry | Claimed paper | DOI | Resolver result |
|-------|--------------|-----|-----------------|
| 26 | Smith & Zhang (2024), quantum AI consciousness | 10.1038/s42256-024-01247-fake | 404 CrossRef, 404 OpenAlex |
| 27 | Doe (2023), post-quantum cryptography | 10.1103/PhysRevLett.131.999999 | 404 CrossRef, 404 OpenAlex |
| 28 | Patel et al. (2024), GPT-5 theory of mind | 10.1016/j.cell.2024.invented | 404 CrossRef, 404 OpenAlex |

## Metadata Mismatches (2)

**Entry 29 — metadata_mismatch_author:** DOI `10.48550/arXiv.1706.03762` resolves to "Attention Is All You Need" by Vaswani et al. (2017). Corpus entry lists first author as "Smith, J." — does not match resolved first author Vaswani.

**Entry 30 — metadata_mismatch_year:** DOI `10.1038/nature14539` resolves to LeCun et al. "Deep learning" (2015). Corpus entry lists year 2020 — diverges by 5 years, exceeding the ±1 tolerance.

## Retracted References

**Entry 32 — RETRACTED: Wakefield et al. (1998).** DOI `10.1016/S0140-6736(97)11096-0` resolves. CrossRef title field is prefixed with "RETRACTED:" and includes a retraction relation pointing to DOI `10.1016/s0140-6736(10)60175-4` (retraction date: 2010-02-06). OpenAlex `is_retracted: true`. **Do not cite as current scientific evidence.** The retraction notice documents ethical violations and manipulated data; the claimed MMR vaccine–autism link has been thoroughly refuted.

## Duplicate Entry

**Entry 31** is an exact duplicate of Entry 1 (Vaswani et al. 2017, DOI `10.48550/arXiv.1706.03762`). Reference ID: `vaswani-2017-attention-is-all-you-need-dup2`. Verdict mirrored from canonical; resolver not re-queried (Step 0.5.5).

## Resolver Outages

| Resolver | Status | Affected entries |
|----------|--------|-----------------|
| CrossRef | 404 for arXiv DOIs (expected behaviour) | 1, 15 (fell through to DataCite) |
| OpenAlex title search | HTTP 503 Service Unavailable | 16, 18, 19, 20, 21, 23, 25 |
| Semantic Scholar title search | HTTP 429 Too Many Requests | 16, 18, 19, 20, 21, 23, 25 |
